reviews.author: Bảo Quang Nguyễn
reviews.text: Nhan Tower (Tháp Nhạn) – The soul of my hometown, Phu Yen. Phu Yen is my hometown, and Nhan Mountain together with the Đà River is the symbol of our province. Whenever people mention Phu Yen, this is the place they think of. Standing at the peak of Nhan Mountain, Nhan Tower looks over the city and carries deep historical and cultural meaning. Built around the 11th century, Nhan Tower is an ancient Cham architectural work and a special national architectural and artistic relic, located at about 64 meters above sea level. The tower has a square layout (about 10 meters per side), a height of 23.5 meters, and a well-balanced structure divided into three parts: base, body, and roof. At the top is a finely carved stone Linga, adding spiritual depth to the site. The main entrance faces east, while the other three sides are decorated with patterns and symbolic false doors. The tower was built from bricks measuring approximately 40 × 20 × 8 cm, laid tightly together. What still amazes people today is that no one fully knows how the ancient Cham builders bonded these bricks; there is no visible cement or mortar, yet the structure has stood firmly for nearly a thousand years. The harmony between materials, architectural lines, and stone carvings gives the tower a feeling of being both solid and graceful. Historical traces show that this area once held a large Cham architectural complex, but today Nhan Tower is the only structure that remains, preserving great historical and artistic value. The site was restored from 1994 to 2000 and officially recognized in 2018 as a Special National Architectural and Artistic Relic. As someone raised in Phu Yen, I truly recommend this place to everyone, locals and foreign travelers alike. Nhan Tower is not just a landmark, but a symbol of pride, mystery, and the timeless spirit of my hometown.

reviews.author: Michael Page
reviews.text: Beautiful views and a stunning tower. Easy walk up if moderately fit. Toilets and some food available. No cost except parking. Also a nice temple and memorial. Well worth the visit any tome but sunrise probably best.

reviews.author: tan SH
reviews.text: It is better to come in the evening, as it will less Sun, and able to see the sunset and the light up of the Temple. The site is Small will not take more than 30min. Buy ticket to get the ride up the hill (but i did not see they check the ticket 😒). It good to stop-by support & pray in this heritage site.

reviews.author: Johann Marjeta
reviews.text: Nice little walk uphill to this well preserved Cham Temple. Place is clean and the view from the top is nice. There is a quite cheap electric shuttle for those who can't or don't want to walk.

reviews.author: PL Lau
reviews.text: A Piece of History Atop a Hill Nhan Temple is a hidden gem in Tuy Hoa, offering a glimpse into Vietnam's rich history. This ancient structure, a remnant of the Cambodian legacy, sits majestically atop a small hill. Nhan Tower is one of the must-visit spiritual and historical destinations in Phu Yen. The ancient architecture and peaceful atmosphere here bring a feeling of peace and tranquility. The intricate architecture and the serene atmosphere create a truly captivating experience. The surrounding scenery is breathtaking, making it a perfect spot for a peaceful stroll or a moment of reflection. Nhan Tower is a great photography spot with unique architecture and a cool green space. The natural light here is very beautiful, making the photos more vivid. I got some very satisfying photos here. I am very impressed with the meticulousness in every detail of the tower. This is truly a unique architectural work with great cultural value. Highly recommend visiting Nhan Temple for a unique and unforgettable experience!

reviews.author: 圖巴克
reviews.text: Forecasting the economic outlook for tourism in Phu Yen, Vietnam, involves considering several key factors: 1. **Infrastructure Development**: Continued investment in transportation and accommodations will likely enhance accessibility, attracting more tourists. 2. **Sustainable Tourism Trends**: Increasing interest in eco-tourism and cultural experiences can position Phu Yen as a desirable destination for both domestic and international travelers. 3. **Government Initiatives**: Supportive policies and promotional campaigns can stimulate tourism growth, especially in the wake of recovery from the pandemic. 4. **Market Demand**: A rise in local and regional travel, alongside the gradual return of international tourists, can boost revenue for the sector. 5. **Challenges**: Issues such as environmental concerns, competition from other destinations, and potential global economic uncertainties could impact growth. Overall, if these factors align positively, Phu Yen could see significant growth in its tourism economy over the next few years

reviews.author: Pham Phuong
reviews.text: Convenient location to see the whole Tuy Hoa. It's a pity that it's not very sunny to take the best photos.

reviews.author: Rainie Green
reviews.text: Popular place in Tuy Hoa City. Free admission. You can walk (620m) or climb up by using stairs. Or electric car ticket with 15k round trip

reviews.author: Nicole Cox
reviews.text: Two meo sightings and a wonderful historical view

reviews.author: Dmitri Aleksandrov
reviews.text: Calm place and peaciful nearby area (almost don't hear city noize) Walking here is easy. You can enjoy some top views from here as well.

reviews.author: Hằng
reviews.text: It’s already 6PM when I came here, and fortunately, it still opened. 15000 VND per person for a tourist coach to go up and go down.

reviews.author: Hye Huang
reviews.text: One of the ancient relics left by the caretakers, not too special compared to the ancient towers of the localities, the Nhan tower tourist area is not too big, but it is worth visiting

reviews.author: Wilson Yap
reviews.text: Apparently there is no entry fee, just the 10k one way/ 15k two way for the car up. As the cart driver had gone for lunch, the guard just waved me in and showed his fingers to walk up. Haha. Walking up took just several minutes. There is a small exhibition room showcasing the discovery of the site and several artifacts. And also a statue garden with numerous sculptures. The temple is quite majestic and the view of the city here is nice. Inside house the goddess Ma Zhu. She is a goddess that is worshipped by many people who lived close to the sea. There's an abandon bunker behind the temple when you go further uphill. Another 5 min walk you can get to the Martyrs Memorial, Phu Yen Province. The scenery here is also good.

reviews.author: Alok B. Singh
reviews.text: Calm and historic. Buy 15.000 Vn ticket and electric cart will take you up to hill.

reviews.author: Violet Manh
reviews.text: This place gives me a feeling that is really hard to explain. Although Thap Nhan mountain is the culture of Thailand, Cambodia or Laos, the origin is still in Vietnam, showing me some past historical events that is so real.! Love here and recommendation if you wanna understand more about history.

reviews.author: Tri Vuong
reviews.text: Nhan temple is the symbol of Tuy Hoa, Phu Vien. Great place with beautiful scenery…

reviews.author: Kent Kuo
reviews.text: Wow…. Amazing brick work over 1000years ago.

reviews.author: Kat Ky
reviews.text: Small and simple with one Champa culture brick tower. It's ok for a quick visit. It's free for walking along, and cost 15.000 vnd for electric bus on round trip.

reviews.author: David Levitch
reviews.text: Breezy place, good for resting after the climb

reviews.author: Paul Ashikanen
reviews.text: One of the best places to visit in Tuy Hoa. There’s an electric car service that leads up to the magnificent Cham temple which is a bit expensive but worth it. The temple is really beautiful and old, and you can play the stone piano at the souvenir shop nearby, which is awesome. Overall, 10/10, HIGHLY RECOMMENDED TO VISIT.

reviews.author: Robert Morris
reviews.text: Cool place to climb to see the city! Especially at night!

reviews.author: Anh Nguyen
reviews.text: Nice place, great view down the city. Well organized, friendly staffs.

reviews.author: Nathaniel Wong
reviews.text: At piece of history from the Cambodian legacy on top of a small hill. Visitors can choose to hike or take the golf cart up the hill. We were quite lucky as the temple inner door was opened during our visit.

reviews.author: Purushotham Sunku
reviews.text: The shiva temple sanctum tower built before the 13th century on a small hilltop infront of the river joining the mighty ocean might have been sacred place for the cham kingdom.Restored structure with shining red bricks everywhere gives us an idea of painful reconstruction though perfect for selfies as a memory of your visit.All the stucco images and designs have perished with the times leaving only traces of Indra sitting on big elephants similar to Po Nagar temple of Nha Trang on three outer walls of sanctum tower.There's beautiful sculpture garden of cham kingdom having excellent stone art in the form of apsara with her immortal youth and beauty,stiff and standing lakshmi,unique and lively carving of garuda,meditative brahma and saraswati,lively goddess with halo like decoration behind the head,immortal nataraja and unique carving of kubera style of shiva,lokapala and Tara makes you happy for visiting the ancient temple complex.

reviews.author: M. Véaigh
reviews.text: I visited back in April, 2019. Definitely worth a look if you're in Tuy Hoa. Not worth a special trip out though, it is just one building. But it is on a very high hill with a nice view of the surrounding city and countryside.

reviews.author: Thao Nguyen
reviews.text: Should visit to explore Champa culture, apart the temple, there are many statues represent for their beliefs. You can walk or use tramcar, 15k/person for 2-way travel.

reviews.author: HSFi
reviews.text: The guard tried to rip me off because he realised I couldn't understand Vietnamese!

reviews.author: Aditya Trighatia
reviews.text: Just a fascinating and beautiful place. Looks gorgeous at night. Don’t miss the sculptures a level below the temple.

reviews.author: dhruv kumar
reviews.text: Very interesting place and felt like this cham is Better than one at Mui ne
